g.text: SUM / AVG return NULL when no values qualify
Mistake: NULL treated as 0 or counted
SQL skips NULLs in column aggregates

div.p: SUM(NULL, NULL, NULL) → NULL (nothing to sum) AVG(NULL, NULL, NULL) → NULL (0 ÷ 0 is undefined) Developers often expect 0 — use COALESCE(SUM(col), 0) to get 0 explicitly.
Error: divides SUM by total row count AVG = SUM(age) / COUNT_ALL = 90 / 5 = 18.0 NULL is not zero — it is the absence of a value. Including NULLs in the denominator is wrong.
Rule: aggregate functions skip NULL values AVG(age) = SUM(age) / COUNT(age) = 90 / 3 = 30.0 COUNT(*) counts rows; COUNT(col) counts non-NULLs. They diverge whenever NULLs are present.
